rulings.Case Title: JOHNA PECOT et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 29, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOHNA PECOT Motion To Vacate. Granted. (CCP sec. 473(b).) The September 26, 2014 Order sustaining the demurrer is vacated. Plaintiff has established that an opposition to the demurrer was not timely filed because Plaintiff mistakenly thought Defendant continued the hearing date. A hearing on the demurrer shall be added to the February 23, 2015 calendar. Plaintiff must timely file the proposed opposition according to the Code of Civil Procedure, contemplating the new hearing date. Defendant may file a reply brief according to the Code of Civil Procedure, contemplating the new hearing date. Courtesy copies of all papers filed, including the moving papers, shall be delivered to Dept. 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JULES RADCLIFFE et al VS. PHUONG PHAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Phuong Pham And Tp Pham, Llc'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas To Third Party Entities And Stay Depositions, And For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 29, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANTS PHUONG PHAM AND TP PHAM, LLC'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas To Third Party Entities And Stay Depositions, And For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Frank Conway, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion is DENIED because the information and records sought by Plaintiffs' subpoenas are directly relevant to defendant's testimony that she was unable to complete rehabilitation work due to financial constraints. Defendant also failed to file a separate statement and meet and confer prior to filing this motion, as required by CRC 3.1345(a) (5), CCP 2025.420, which apply to this motion. Naser v. Lakeridge Athletic Club (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 571, 577. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to FConway@partonsell.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JEAN-PIERRE BANOWICZ VS. NOE'S NEST, INC. DBA NOE'S NEST BED AND BREAKFAST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary And Terminating Sanctions For Abuse Of Discovery Process
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 29, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT NOE'S NEST, INC. DBA NOE'S NEST BED AND BREAKFAST's Motion For Monetary And Terminating Sanctions For Abuse Of Discovery Process. Pro Tem Judge Frank Conway, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion is DENIED IN PART and Plaintiff given one last chance to comply with CCP 2025.010 et seq. Within 10 days after entry of the order on this motion, Plaintiff is ordered to: (1) appear for his deposition and (2) pay Defendant's counsel $941.60 in CCP 2023.030(a) monetary sanctions, consisting of $221.60 prior sanctions plus $720 reasonable attorney's fees and costs for this motion (4 hrs. at $150 per hr. and $120 filing fees). Plaintiff is admonished that, in the event he fails to comply with this order without substantial justification, the Court will be inclined to grant a future CCP 2023.030(d)(3) motion for terminating sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to FConway@partonsell.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FEI SU VS. SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Plaintiff Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 29, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF FEI SU Motion For Order Granting Plaintiff Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Granted. Plaintiff shall file her proposed amended complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. "The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified." (Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422.) Defendant has not shown it will be unduly prejudiced by the amendment. The trial date is set for approximately six months from now and discovery is ongoing. Defendant will have sufficient time to file a demurrer or motion for summary judgment and to conduct discovery on the new allegations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: JACK K FRAZIER VS. MICHAEL QUINN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Deposition Questions And Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 29, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Answers To Deposition Questions And Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney. Pro Tem Judge Frank Conway, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff is ordered to attend a further deposition and answer all questions identified in the separate statement and reasonably related follow up questions. Defendant's request for sanctions is DENIED due to the lack of "serious effort at negotiation and informal resolution" following the deposition during which the meet and confer discussion was limited to "verbal sparring over the propriety of objections." Townsend v. Superior Court (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 1431. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to FConway@partonsell.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PRAVIN PATEL et al VS. SHARAD SHANTILAL PATEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling On Anti-Slapp Motion And/Or Renewal Of Anti-Slapp Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 29, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT VINAY PATEL Motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling On Anti-Slapp Motion And/Or Renewal Of Anti-Slapp Motion. The Court grants Defendants' Motion for Reconsideration and, upon reconsideration, denies Defendants' Anti-SLAPP Motion to Strike. Defendants have failed to establish that the activity from which Plaintiffs' challenged causes of action arise is protected activity. Defendants contended in the initial motion and again here that the activity was protected because it was activity connected with "legally authorized official proceeding[s]," and that the newspaper announcements were part of an investigation "undertaken in anticipation of civil litigation." (Defendants' Memo. Points & Authorities at 8:8-12.) However, CCP § 425.16 "clearly limits the petitioning or free speech activity [that is protected] to that made pursuant to rights granted by the United States or California Constitutions, and neither Constitution grants a United States citizen the right to petition a foreign government." (Guessous v. Chrome Hearts LLC (2009) 179 Cal.App.4th 1177, 1185.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: C. OVERAA & CO. VS. U.S. GLASS & ALUMINUM, BENDHEIM WALL SYSTEMS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Contest The Good Faith Of The Settlement Of Vbn Corporation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 29, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT U.S. GLASS & ALUMINUM, INC. Motion To Contest The Good Faith Of The Settlement Of Vbn Corporation. Granted. Defendant VBN Corp. ("VBN") has not sufficiently established that its settlement with Plaintiff is in good faith. Plaintiff seeks to recover $1.4 million in damages, yet VBN would be settling out of the case for only $7,500. Defendant U.S. Glass & Aluminum ("U.S. Glass") has presented evidence that VBN, the architect of record for the project, fell below the standard of care, which contributed to the failure of the channel glass panels. (See, e.g., Declaration of Jill Lifter, Ex. E., Castle Depo., p. 116:1-16, Plaintiff's expert testifying that VBN should not have stamped architectural drawings that did not include a requirement for sealing the wires in the channel glass system; Declaration of Jill Lifer, Ex. B, Nethers Depo., pp. 116:5-19, 196:17-197:8, Plaintiff's employee testifying that VBN prepared the drawings and plans for the channel glass system.) As such, VBN has not established that $7,500 represents its proportionate liability to Plaintiff's potential damages. Further, VBN does not provide any information about its financial condition and insurance policy limits, if any, and therefore the Court cannot consider this factor in determining whether the settlement is in good faith. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN A. CULVER VS. CONRADO AMADOR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 29, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT CONRADO AMADOR DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The matter is taken off calendar. Amended complaint filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)
